description:
Goal: move remaining frontend sugar out of body lowering and the old macro pass by making it source-production work over compiler2-owned AST.

Signal:
- `fixtures/pipe_headless_case` runs through `fz2` without old frontend desugaring
- representative capture/lambda sugar fixtures run through `fz2`: `&(&1 + &2)`, bare `&1`, `&name/arity`, and guarded/multi-clause lambdas become ordinary lowered forms before body lowering
- operator sugars (`++`, `--`, `<>`, ranges, `in`, `not in`) enter downstream as ordinary calls/forms with correct namespace/env context
- body lowering no longer rejects `Capture`/`CaptureArg` because those source-only forms do not reach it

Change:
- implement source-production rewrites or built-in macros for pipe/headless case, capture placeholders, multi-clause lambdas, and operator sugars
- preserve spans and lexical context through rewrites
- keep structured control/data lowering owned by the existing compiler2 body/dispatch pipeline; this ticket only removes source-only sugar before that seam
- add fixture/telemetry assertions that prove fz2 did not call old frontend macro/desugar paths

Why:
- source sugar is not runtime semantics and not backend work
- once quoted source production exists, these rewrites belong beside macro expansion where returned AST is recursively normalized
title: Compiler2 source sugars: define pipe, capture, operators, and lambda rewrites in source production
